titleStmt.title: SII 15.10: original edition by P. B. Desai, Dines Chandra Sircar and G. S. Gai
No. 10. (B.K. No. 76 of 1926-27.) SORAṬŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a broken slab set up in front of the Kallēśvara temple.

The record seems to belong to the reign of Chāḷukya Tribhuvanamalla (Vikramāditya VI). It seems to refer to Soṟaṭūr as being governed by the goddess Mahālakshmī of Kolhāpur (cf. South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XI, Part II, No. 137). The Two Hundred Mahājanas of Soṟaṭūr are stated to have installed (an image of) the god Vishṇu. The other details are lost, the epigraph being fragmentary.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
jayatyāviṣkṛtaṁ viṣṇōrvvārāhaṁ kṣōbhitārṇnavaṁ dakṣiṇōnnata daṁṣṭrāgra viśrāṁ The slab is broken on
svasti samastabhuvanāśraya śrīpṛthvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja
paramabhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśrayakuḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyābharaṇaṁ śrīmattri
dēvara vijayarājyamuttarōttarābhivṛddhipravarddhamānamācandrārkkatā
mire . vṛtta . trijagadbyāpitakīrttisapta satatīrtthasnānasaṁpūta nīraja The wordsapta is written below the line.
hini mahāśaktisvarūpākhya yuddhajayaśrīprade sarbbaśāśvate
vinamnajanābhīṣṭa varapradāyi sūṟaṭūraṁ pāḷisutti
śṟutigaḷoḷella pariṇataraniṁdyaramaḷayaśaśrīvallabha
sarbbaśāstravidarinnūrvvar . v® . vihitācāravicā
duṣṭanigraha śiṣṭapratipāḷakar pṛthuya
soṟaṭūra vipraru Ucitajñarnnīti
tiprakara pārāvāra pārāyaṇa
budhapriyaṁ parahitārttha cāru
bhāsvatkīrtti viṣṇupratiṣṭhaya
priyadiṁ māḍida nāya
yyaṁ janakaṁ guṇi
siyaṇnanuṁ dhare The
Digital edition of SII 15.10 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
